title: T-007 — x-site-id Next.js middleware + JWT claim wiring (§11.4 V-MS-15..17, §6.2)
prompt:
# T-007 — x-site-id middleware + JWT claim wiring

PRD: docs/prd/14-MULTI-SITE-PRD.md §6.2 data flow, §11.4 V-MS-15..17
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Wire the request-side surface for site context. Every authenticated request goes through Next.js middleware that determines `site_id`, validates it against `site_user_access`, writes (or refreshes) the `app.session_site_contexts` row, and surfaces V-MS-15/16/17 error codes deterministically.

## Implementation contract
1. New file `apps/web/middleware.ts` (or append to existing) — middleware step `siteContextMiddleware` that:
   - Reads `x-site-id` header; if absent, falls back to httpOnly cookie `ms_site_id` (set by T-020 site switcher).
   - If user has exactly one active `site_user_access` row → auto-select that site (V-MS-17), skip header check.
   - If user is super_admin → ALL-sites mode allowed (write session_site_contexts row with site_id NULL).
   - If user has multiple sites and header missing → return 400 SITE_CONTEXT_REQUIRED (V-MS-15).
   - If header references a site not in caller's site_user_access → return 403 SITE_ACCESS_DENIED (V-MS-16); audit log entry written.
   - On success, upsert `app.session_site_contexts(session_token, user_id, org_id, site_id, created_at=now())` and pass site_id forward via Next.js request headers for Server Components to read. Server Actions downstream use app.current_site_id() (never raw GUC) to read site context from the session.
2. Helper `apps/web/lib/auth/get-site-context.ts` — Server Component-side reader returning typed `{ sessionToken, orgId, siteId, userId }` for use in withSiteContext calls.
3. JWT claim wiring: Supabase session callback adds `accessible_site_ids: string[]` claim from `site_user_access WHERE user_id = $1 AND active = true` to avoid a DB hit per request — middleware compares header against claim before doing DB upsert.
4. Tests (vitest): all 5 paths — auto-select single-site, super_admin ALL, multi-site missing-header 400, multi-site mismatch 403, multi-site valid header 200 + cookie written.

## Files
- apps/web/middleware.ts [modify]
- apps/web/lib/auth/site-context-middleware.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/auth/get-site-context.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/auth/__tests__/site-context-middleware.test.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/supabase/server.ts [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
<!-- AC count consolidated from 6→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost) -->
1. Given user has 1 active site_user_access row, when GET /any-page is invoked without x-site-id header, then middleware auto-selects that site (V-MS-17) and writes app.session_site_contexts; response is 200.
2. Given user has 3 active site_user_access rows and request has no x-site-id and no cookie, when middleware runs, then 400 SITE_CONTEXT_REQUIRED is returned (V-MS-15) and no app.session_site_contexts row is written.
3. Given user has site_user_access at site_X only, when request includes x-site-id=site_Y, then 403 SITE_ACCESS_DENIED is returned (V-MS-16); audit_log gets a row with action='site_access_denied', actor=user_id, ref=site_Y.
4. Given super_admin requests without x-site-id, when middleware runs, then app.session_site_contexts row is written with site_id NULL (ALL-sites) and response is 200.
5. Given a valid request 200, when Server Component calls getSiteContext(), then it returns the same site_id middleware decided (proven by integration test reading from request headers).
6. Given JWT lacks accessible_site_ids claim, when middleware runs, then it falls back to a single DB read of site_user_access and surfaces claim staleness in observability (T-117).

## Test strategy
- RED: vitest assert all 5 routing paths (auto-select, super_admin, missing-header 400, mismatch 403, valid 200) + JWT claim fallback path before middleware exists.
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/lib/auth/__tests__/site-context-middleware.test.ts'.

## Risk red lines
- Do not trust x-site-id without comparing to accessible_site_ids — header is attacker-controlled.
- Do not hit the DB on every request to read site_user_access — use JWT claim primed by Supabase session callback; only fall back on claim-miss.
- Do not write app.session_site_contexts on the 403 path — that would silently widen the access window.
- Do not block super_admin without an explicit allow — V-MS-07 documented bypass.
- Do not log session_token in plaintext — re-use observability redact allowlist (T-117).

pipeline_inputs.description: Next.js middleware reads x-site-id header (cookie fallback), validates against caller's site_user_access, writes app.session_site_contexts row, and surfaces SITE_CONTEXT_REQUIRED / SITE_ACCESS_DENIED per V-MS-15/16; auto-selects primary_site for single-site users per V-MS-17.
pipeline_inputs.details: Next.js middleware reads x-site-id header (cookie fallback), validates against caller's site_user_access, writes app.session_site_contexts row, and surfaces SITE_CONTEXT_REQUIRED / SITE_ACCESS_DENIED per V-MS-15/16; auto-selects primary_site for single-site users per V-MS-17. Scope: Wire the request-side surface for site context. Touches: apps/web/middleware.ts, apps/web/lib/auth/site-context-middleware.ts, apps/web/lib/auth/get-site-context.ts, apps/web/lib/auth/__tests__/site-context-middleware.test.ts. Constraints: 1. New file `apps/web/middleware.ts` (or append to existing) — middleware step `siteContextMiddleware` that: - Reads `x-site-id` header; if absent, falls back to httpOnly cookie `ms_site_id` (set by T-020 site switcher). - If user has exactly one active `site_user_access` row → auto-select that site (V-MS-17), skip header check. - If user is super_. out_of_scope: Do not implement the site switcher UI (T-020).
Do not implement the cookie write side (T-020 site switcher writes the cookie).
Do not implement audit table — re-use audit_events from foundation.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Re-uses Supabase auth session callback established by foundation T-011.
Observability redact allowlist; never log session_token plaintext.

Cross-mod dep to 00-foundation T-125 declared (withOrgContext + app.current_org_id() per F11 rule). withSiteContext HOF composes on top of T-125; middleware writes session_site_contexts using withSiteContext.

acceptance_criteria: Given user has 1 active site_user_access row, when GET /any-page invoked without x-site-id header, then middleware auto-selects that site (V-MS-17) and writes app.session_site_contexts; response is 200.

Given user has 3 active site_user_access rows and request has no x-site-id and no cookie, when middleware runs, then 400 SITE_CONTEXT_REQUIRED returned (V-MS-15); no session_site_contexts row written; AND given super_admin requests without x-site-id header, when middleware runs, then app.session_site_contexts records NULL (ALL-sites); getSiteContext() returns same site_id in every Server Component for the request.
Given user has site_user_access at site_X only, when request includes x-site-id=site_Y, then 403 SITE_ACCESS_DENIED returned (V-MS-16); audit_events row written.
Given JWT lacks accessible_site_ids claim, when middleware runs, then falls back to a single DB read and surfaces claim staleness in observability.
test_strategy: RED: vitest all 5 routing paths + JWT claim fallback before middleware exists.
Verify: 403 SITE_ACCESS_DENIED when request includes x-site-id for a site not in user's site_user_access (original AC3); super_admin NULL site context recorded (original AC4).
risk_red_lines: Do not trust x-site-id without comparing to accessible_site_ids JWT claim.
Do not hit DB on every request — use JWT claim with claim-miss fallback.
Do not write session_site_contexts on 403 path.
Do not block super_admin (V-MS-07 documented bypass).
Do not log session_token plaintext — re-use T-117 redact allowlist.
